items.scenario: You are a third-year biology student working on a research project for Professor Reed's class. Yesterday, the microscope reserved for your lab session broke down halfway through the experiment, and the replacement will not arrive until next week. Your project report is due this Friday.
items.direction: Write an email to Professor Reed. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe what happened with the lab equipment
Explain why the failure affects your report
Request an extension on the submission deadline
items.subject: Request for assignment deadline extension due to lab equipment failure

items.scenario: You are a graduate student who works ten hours a week at a campus marketing office. Your supervisor, Ms. Patel, asked you to provide written feedback on the new internal project tracking tool that your team has been testing for the past month. Several coworkers have privately complained that it is hard to use.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Patel. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the main difficulties your team has noticed
Suggest two practical changes to the tracking tool
Mention which features are working well so far
items.subject: Feedback on the new internal project tracking tool

items.scenario: Your local community center recently shortened its evening hours, closing at six in the afternoon instead of nine. As a regular volunteer at the weekly conversation club for new residents, you have noticed that most attendees can only come after their workday ends.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Owens, the community center director. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how the schedule change affects the conversation club
Provide examples of attendees who can no longer participate
Suggest a partial restoration of the evening hours
items.subject: Concerns about the new community center evening hours

items.scenario: You subscribed to a weekly meal kit service two months ago at a fixed monthly rate. For the last three billing cycles, you have been charged twice in the same week, and customer support has only refunded the duplicate charge once after several days of waiting.
items.direction: Write an email to the customer care team. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the pattern of duplicate charges you have noticed
Explain why the previous resolution was not sufficient
Request a full audit of your billing history and a permanent fix
items.subject: Recurring double-charging issue with my meal subscription
